| `opencode/prompts/omg-workflow.md`           | `.opencode/prompts/omg-workflow.md`       |

**R3.3** The setup command creates any missing directories (`.opencode/commands/`,
`.opencode/agents/`, `.opencode/skills/omg-commands/`, etc.).

**R3.4** If a destination file already exists, overwrite it without prompting.
This supports upgrading to newer versions of the package.

**R3.5** The setup command writes `.workflow.yaml` with the following content:

```yaml
specs:
  directory: docs/specs
```

If `.workflow.yaml` already exists, overwrite it.

**R3.6** The setup command prints a summary of what was copied, the package

**R3.9** If the user runs the CLI with no arguments or an unrecognized

### R4: Shipped file content (rename and update references)

All shipped files must be renamed from the `bd-` prefix to `omg-` and all

**R4.1** File renames (command prefix `bd-` → `omg-`, skill prefix
`beads-`/`adr-` → `omg-`, prompt prefix `beads-` → `omg-`):

| `bd-decomposer.md`          | `omg-decomposer.md`       |
| `beads-commands/SKILL.md`   | `omg-commands/SKILL.md`   |
| `beads-epics/SKILL.md`      | `omg-epics/SKILL.md`      |
| `beads-workflow.md`         | `omg-workflow.md`         |
| *(new)* `omg-build.md`      | `omg-build.md`            |

**R4.2** Internal reference updates (these are string replacements within
file contents — the `bd` CLI commands like `bd create`, `bd sync`, etc.
must NOT be renamed):

| Old reference              | New reference              | Where it appears                              |
|----------------------------|----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| `agent: build`             | `agent: omg-build`         | `omg-work.md`                                 |
| `agent: bd-spec-writer`    | `agent: omg-spec-writer`   | `omg-spec.md`, `omg-spec-refine.md`           |
| `agent: bd-decomposer`    | `agent: omg-decomposer`   | `omg-decompose.md`                            |
| *(new)* `omg-spec-track.md`| `agent: omg-spec-writer`   | `omg-spec-track.md` (add agent field)         |
| `@bd-reviewer`             | `@omg-reviewer`            | `omg-work.md`, `omg-decompose.md`, `omg-epics/SKILL.md` |
| `/bd-spec-refine`          | `/omg-spec-refine`         | `omg-spec.md`, `omg-spec-track.md`            |
| `/bd-decompose`            | `/omg-decompose`           | `omg-spec.md`, `omg-spec-track.md`, `omg-spec-refine.md` |
| `beads-commands` (skill)   | `omg-commands`             | `omg-work.md`, `omg-workflow.md`, agents       |
| `beads-epics` (skill)      | `omg-epics`                | `omg-work.md`, `omg-workflow.md`, agents       |
| `{file:../prompts/beads-workflow.md}` | `{file:../prompts/omg-workflow.md}` | All shipped agents |

**R4.3** Remove all ADR-related references from shipped files:
- Remove the `adr-workflow` skill reference from `omg-workflow.md`
- Remove any ADR skill loading instructions from agents

**R4.4** The `omg-workflow.md` prompt must reference the renamed skills
(`omg-commands`, `omg-epics`) and omit the `adr-workflow` skill.

**R4.5** Add `agent: omg-spec-writer` to the frontmatter of `omg-spec-track.md`
(the original `bd-spec-track.md` had no agent field).

### R5: New `omg-build` agent

**R5.1** Create an `omg-build.md` agent definition that mirrors the behavior
of OpenCode's built-in `build` agent. This is a general-purpose coding agent
with full tool access. The agent definition must include:

- `description`: General-purpose coding agent for the OMG workflow
- `mode: primary`
- `tools`: full access (bash, read, write, edit, glob, grep, task, webfetch,
  skill, todowrite)
- `permission: bash:allow`
- Include `{file:../prompts/omg-workflow.md}` for beads context

**R5.2** The `omg-work.md` command must reference `agent: omg-build` instead
of `agent: build`.

### R6: Shipped file storage in the package

**R6.1** The shipped markdown files (commands, agents, skills, prompts) must
be stored in an `opencode/` directory at the package root, organized as:

```
opencode/
  commands/
    omg-work.md
    omg-spec.md
    omg-spec-track.md
    omg-spec-refine.md
    omg-decompose.md
    omg-status.md
    omg-cleanup.md
  agents/
    omg-build.md
    omg-spec-writer.md
    omg-reviewer.md
    omg-decomposer.md
  skills/
    omg-commands/
      SKILL.md
    omg-epics/
      SKILL.md
  prompts/
    omg-workflow.md
```

**R6.2** The `opencode/` directory must be included in the `files` array in
`package.json` so it ships with the npm package.

### R7: HelloWorldPlugin removal

**R7.1** Delete `src/plugins/hello-world.ts`.

**R7.2** Remove the `HelloWorldPlugin` and `HelloWorldPluginConfig` exports
from `src/index.ts`.

### R8: Local development wrapper update

**R8.1** Update `.opencode/plugins/beads.ts` to be a thin wrapper that imports
`BeadsPlugin` from `../../dist/index.js`, matching the pattern of
`.opencode/plugins/then-chaining.ts`.

**R8.2** The local `.opencode/` commands, agents, skills, and prompts used for
development of this repo should continue to use the `bd-` prefix (they are
the development originals, not the shipped copies). The shipped `omg-` prefixed
files live in the `opencode/` directory at the package root.

### R9: Package.json updates

**R9.1** Add `"bin"` entry with key `@toady00/open-mardi-gras` pointing
to `dist/cli/setup.js`.

**R9.2** Add `opencode/` to the `"files"` array.

**R9.3** Update the README to document:
- `BeadsPlugin` usage and configuration
- Prerequisites: `bd` and `yq` must be on `$PATH`
- The setup command invocation
- That `bd` and `yq` are only required if using `BeadsPlugin`
  (ThenChainingPlugin has no external dependencies)

---

## Acceptance Criteria

### Plugin Runtime

- [ ] `import { BeadsPlugin } from '@toady00/open-mardi-gras'` resolves without error
- [ ] `BeadsPlugin()` returns a valid Plugin that OpenCode can load
- [ ] First user message in a session triggers `bd prime` injection with
      `<beads-context>` tags
- [ ] Subsequent messages in the same session do not re-inject
- [ ] `session.compacted` event triggers re-injection
- [ ] `session.idle` event triggers `bd sync` quietly (no visible message)
- [ ] If `bd prime` returns empty output, no injection occurs

### Plugin Conflict Resolution

- [ ] Running a command with a `then` chain while BeadsPlugin is active
      completes the full chain without beads context being injected mid-chain
- [ ] The beads `bd sync` on `session.idle` still runs during then-chains
      (it's safe — no visible output)
- [ ] After a then-chain completes, beads context injection resumes normally
- [ ] If compaction occurs during a then-chain, beads re-injection is deferred
      until the chain completes, then fires
- [ ] A user-interrupted chain allows beads injection to resume immediately
- [ ] The test-then-command → test-then-prompt chain (the exact scenario from
      the conflict export) completes without beads context interleaving
- [ ] The `PluginCoordinator` works when only ThenChainingPlugin is loaded
- [ ] The `PluginCoordinator` works when only BeadsPlugin is loaded
- [ ] Multiple compactions during a single chain result in only one
      beads re-injection

### CLI Setup

- [ ] The setup command copies all 14 files to the correct `.opencode/`
      subdirectories
- [ ] Missing directories are created automatically
- [ ] Existing files are overwritten without prompting
- [ ] `.workflow.yaml` is written with `specs.directory: docs/specs`
- [ ] Summary of actions and package version is printed to stdout
- [ ] `npm pack` produces a tarball containing the `opencode/` directory
      with all 14 files
- [ ] Running the CLI with no arguments prints usage and exits with code 1

### File Content

- [ ] No shipped file contains the string `bd-` as a command/agent/skill
      prefix (only as CLI commands like `bd create`)
- [ ] No shipped file references `adr-workflow`, `bd-v2-`, or any V2 content
- [ ] All `agent:` frontmatter values reference `omg-` prefixed agents
- [ ] All `/` command references use `omg-` prefix
- [ ] All skill name references use `omg-` prefix
- [ ] All `{file:}` prompt includes reference `omg-workflow.md`
- [ ] Skill SKILL.md frontmatter `name:` fields match their directory names
- [ ] `omg-spec-track.md` has `agent: omg-spec-writer` in frontmatter
- [ ] `omg-work.md` references `agent: omg-build`

### New Agent

- [ ] `omg-build.md` exists in `opencode/agents/`
- [ ] `omg-build.md` includes `{file:../prompts/omg-workflow.md}`
- [ ] `omg-build.md` has full tool access and bash permissions

### Removals

- [ ] `HelloWorldPlugin` is no longer exported
- [ ] `src/plugins/hello-world.ts` does not exist

### Local Development

- [ ] `.opencode/plugins/beads.ts` imports from `../../dist/index.js`
- [ ] The local beads plugin still works after `bun run build`
- [ ] Local `.opencode/` files still use `bd-` prefix (unchanged)

### Build & Quality

- [ ] `bun run build` succeeds
- [ ] `bun run lint` passes
- [ ] `bun test` passes
- [ ] README documents BeadsPlugin prerequisites and setup command
- [ ] `dist/cli/setup.js` starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node`

---

## Edge Cases

### Plugin coordination

- **Chain starts, compaction happens, chain ends**: The queued beads
  re-injection must fire after the chain completes. If another compaction
  happens before the queued injection fires, only one injection should occur
  (not two).

- **Multiple concurrent sessions**: The coordination state must be per-session.
  A then-chain in session A must not block beads injection in session B.

- **Plugin load order**: The coordination mechanism must work regardless of
  which plugin initializes first. Both plugins import from a shared module,
  so load order is irrelevant.

- **Only one plugin active**: If a user only uses `ThenChainingPlugin` without
  `BeadsPlugin` (or vice versa), each plugin must work independently without
  errors. The shared coordination module must handle the case where only one
  side is present.

### Setup command

- **Existing `.opencode/` with other files**: The setup command only writes
  specific files. It must not delete or modify any files it doesn't own.

---

## Deferred to Follow-Up Spec

The following items are explicitly deferred to a separate "hardening" spec
to be created after this integration is working:

- **Dependency detection**: Lazy PATH checks for `bd` and `yq`, warning
  on missing dependencies, disabling plugin when dependencies are absent
- **Non-interactive setup**: `--yes`/`--no-workflow` flags, TTY detection,
  CI-friendly defaults
- **Version mismatch detection**: Checking that plugin runtime version
  matches the version of setup files installed in `.opencode/`
- **Graceful degradation**: `bd` disappearing mid-session, transient errors,
  retry logic
- **Per-session vs. global disabling**: Whether dependency failures disable
  the plugin globally or per-session

---

## Out of Scope

- V2 commands, agents, and skills (ADR-aware variants)
- ADR workflow (commands, agents, `adr-workflow` skill)
- Plugin configuration options (future: selective feature activation)
- Programmatic command/agent registration via the OpenCode `config` hook
  (may be explored in the future if OpenCode supports it)
- Conditional/parallel then-chaining
- Automatic `bd init` or beads project setup

---

## Open Questions

None — all questions resolved during spec refinement.

{"description":"## What

Update the ThenChainingPlugin to register its ChainStateManager with the PluginCoordinator and call `notifyChainComplete()` at the right lifecycle points.

## Why

The ThenChainingPlugin must participate in coordination so the BeadsPlugin knows when chains are active and when they complete. Without this, beads context injection can race with chain advancement (the original bug described in spec R2).

## Implementation

### Register ChainStateManager (spec R2.5):
In `src/plugins/then-chaining.ts`, after creating the `ChainStateManager` (line 37), call:
```typescript
import { coordinator } from '../coordination.js'
coordinator.registerChainState(stateManager)
```

### Notify on chain completion (spec R2.7):
In `src/plugins/then-chaining.ts`, in the `event` handler for `session.idle` (around line 177), after `executor.processNext(sessionID)` returns `false`, call:
```typescript
if (!dispatched) {
  coordinator.notifyChainComplete(sessionID)
  await logger('info', 'Then chain: completed')
}
```

### Notify on chain interruption (spec R2.7):
In `src/plugins/then-chaining.ts`, at every call site where `stateManager.interrupt(sessionID)` is called (lines 107 and 138), add `coordinator.notifyChainComplete(sessionID)` immediately after. There are two interrupt sites:
1. Line ~107: user command with no then-chain interrupts active chain
2. Line ~138: user command with a then-chain interrupts and replaces active chain

**Important**: Do NOT modify `src/then-chaining/state.ts` or `src/then-chaining/executor.ts` to add coordinator calls. The coordinator is a plugin-level concern — keep it in the plugin file where the call sites already exist. The state and executor modules should remain coordinator-unaware.

### Safety:
`notifyChainComplete()` is a no-op when no callbacks are queued, so it's safe to call unconditionally. No conditional logic needed.

## Where

- Modified: `src/plugins/then-chaining.ts` (register chain state + notify calls)
- NOT modified: `src/then-chaining/executor.ts`, `src/then-chaining/state.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ChainStateManager is registered with coordinator during plugin init
- [ ] `notifyChainComplete()` called when `processNext()` returns false (chain done)
- [ ] `notifyChainComplete()` called after each `stateManager.interrupt()` call
- [ ] Existing then-chaining tests still pass (`bun test`)
- [ ] ThenChainingPlugin works correctly when BeadsPlugin is NOT loaded (coordinator has no queued callbacks → no-op)
- [ ] `bun run build` succeeds"}

{"description":"## What

Create `src/coordination.ts` exporting a `PluginCoordinator` class and a module-level singleton `coordinator`.

## Why

Both BeadsPlugin and ThenChainingPlugin need to coordinate around session events. The coordinator is the shared dependency that both plugins import. It must exist before either plugin can reference it.

## Implementation

Create `src/coordination.ts` with the following API:

```typescript
// Interface for what the coordinator needs from a chain state manager.
// Avoids importing the concrete ChainStateManager class, keeping the
// coordination module decoupled from then-chaining internals.
export interface ChainStateProvider {
  hasActiveChain(sessionID: string): boolean
}

export class PluginCoordinator {
  registerChainState(provider: ChainStateProvider): void
  isChainActive(sessionID: string): boolean
  onChainComplete(sessionID: string, callback: () => void): void
  notifyChainComplete(sessionID: string): void
}

export const coordinator: PluginCoordinator
```

### Key behaviors (spec R2.5–R2.8):

- `registerChainState(provider)`: Stores a reference to the provider. Called by ThenChainingPlugin during init. The `ChainStateManager` class already satisfies the `ChainStateProvider` interface (it has `hasActiveChain(sessionID)`).
- `isChainActive(sessionID)`: Queries all registered providers to check if any chain is active for the given session. Returns `false` if no providers are registered (safe when only BeadsPlugin is loaded).
- `onChainComplete(sessionID, callback)`: Queues a callback to fire when `notifyChainComplete` is called for that session. **Deduplicates**: if a callback is already queued for a session, replace it (don't stack). This handles R2.8 (multiple compactions during a chain → only one re-injection).
- `notifyChainComplete(sessionID)`: Fires and removes all queued callbacks for the session. Must be a no-op when no callbacks are queued (safe to call unconditionally).

### Internal state:
- `private providers: ChainStateProvider[]` — registered chain state providers
- `private pendingCallbacks: Map<string, () => void>` — one callback per session (deduplication)

### Coordination state is per-session:
A then-chain in session A must not block beads injection in session B. The `isChainActive()` and callback maps are keyed by sessionID.

### Export from `src/index.ts`:
Add: `export { coordinator, PluginCoordinator } from './coordination.js'`
Also export the `ChainStateProvider` type.

## Where

- New file: `src/coordination.ts`
- Modified: `src/index.ts` (add exports)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `coordinator` is a module-level singleton (both plugins get the same instance)
- [ ] `ChainStateProvider` interface is exported (not the concrete ChainStateManager)
- [ ] `isChainActive()` returns `false` when no providers are registered
- [ ] `onChainComplete()` deduplicates callbacks per session (R2.8)
- [ ] `notifyChainComplete()` is a no-op when no callbacks are queued
- [ ] Per-session isolation: chain in session A doesn't affect session B
- [ ] Unit tests in `src/coordination.test.ts` cover all methods and edge cases
- [ ] `bun run build` succeeds"}

{"description":"## What

Create the CLI entry point at `src/cli/setup.ts` that copies shipped opencode files into the user's project `.opencode/` directory.

## Why

Users need a single command (`npx @toady00/open-mardi-gras setup`) to install all the workflow files (commands, agents, skills, prompts) into their project. This is the primary onboarding mechanism for the beads workflow.

## Implementation

### Entry point (spec R3.1, R3.9):
`src/cli/setup.ts` is the main CLI script. It parses `process.argv`:
- If first arg is `setup`, run the setup logic
- Otherwise (no args or unrecognized arg), print usage message and `process.exit(1)`

### Setup logic (spec R3.2–R3.6):

1. **Resolve paths**: The package's `opencode/` directory is at `path.resolve(__dirname, '../../opencode')` (relative to `dist/cli/setup.js`). The destination is `path.resolve(process.cwd(), '.opencode')`.

2. **Copy files** (R3.2): Copy all 14 files from the package's `opencode/` tree to the project's `.opencode/` tree, preserving subdirectory structure:
   - `opencode/commands/*` → `.opencode/commands/*`
   - `opencode/agents/*` → `.opencode/agents/*`
   - `opencode/skills/omg-commands/SKILL.md` → `.opencode/skills/omg-commands/SKILL.md`
   - `opencode/skills/omg-epics/SKILL.md` → `.opencode/skills/omg-epics/SKILL.md`
   - `opencode/prompts/*` → `.opencode/prompts/*`

3. **Create directories** (R3.3): Use `fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })` before each copy.

4. **Overwrite existing** (R3.4): Use `fs.copyFileSync()` which overwrites by default.

5. **Write .workflow.yaml** (R3.5): Write to `path.resolve(process.cwd(), '.workflow.yaml')`:
   ```yaml
   specs:
     directory: docs/specs
   ```

6. **Print summary** (R3.6): Print each file copied, the package version (read from `package.json` via `path.resolve(__dirname, '../../package.json')`), and next steps: "Add BeadsPlugin() to your opencode config".

### Edge case (spec Edge Cases → Setup):
The setup command only writes specific files. It must NOT delete or modify any files it doesn't own in `.opencode/`.

### Note:
The `package.json` changes (bin entry, build script shebang step, files array) are handled in the "Package.json updates" task (omg-lkv.8). This task only creates the TypeScript source file.

## Where

- New file: `src/cli/setup.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/cli/setup.ts` compiles without errors
- [ ] Setup logic copies all 14 files to correct `.opencode/` subdirectories
- [ ] Missing directories are created automatically
- [ ] Existing files are overwritten without prompting
- [ ] `.workflow.yaml` is written with `specs.directory: docs/specs`
- [ ] Summary of actions and package version printed to stdout
- [ ] Running CLI with no arguments prints usage and exits with code 1
- [ ] `bun run build` succeeds"}

### Entry point (spec R3.1, R3.7, R3.9):
`src/cli/setup.ts` is the main CLI script. It parses `process.argv`:
- If first arg is `setup`, run the setup logic
- Otherwise (no args or unrecognized arg), print usage message and `process.exit(1)`

### Setup logic (spec R3.2–R3.6):

1. **Resolve paths**: The package's `opencode/` directory is at `path.resolve(__dirname, '../../opencode')` (relative to `dist/cli/setup.js`). The destination is `path.resolve(process.cwd(), '.opencode')`.

2. **Copy files** (R3.2): Copy all 14 files from the package's `opencode/` tree to the project's `.opencode/` tree, preserving subdirectory structure:
   - `opencode/commands/*` → `.opencode/commands/*`
   - `opencode/agents/*` → `.opencode/agents/*`
   - `opencode/skills/omg-commands/SKILL.md` → `.opencode/skills/omg-commands/SKILL.md`
   - `opencode/skills/omg-epics/SKILL.md` → `.opencode/skills/omg-epics/SKILL.md`
   - `opencode/prompts/*` → `.opencode/prompts/*`

3. **Create directories** (R3.3): Use `fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })` before each copy.

4. **Overwrite existing** (R3.4): Use `fs.copyFileSync()` which overwrites by default.

5. **Write .workflow.yaml** (R3.5): Write to `path.resolve(process.cwd(), '.workflow.yaml')`:
   ```yaml
   specs:
     directory: docs/specs
   ```

6. **Print summary** (R3.6): Print each file copied, the package version (read from `package.json`), and next steps: "Add BeadsPlugin() to your opencode config".

### Build integration (spec R3.8):
The compiled `dist/cli/setup.js` must start with `#!/usr/bin/env node`. Since `tsc` doesn't emit shebangs, add a post-build step to the `build` script in `package.json`:
```
"build": "tsc -p tsconfig.build.json && echo '#!/usr/bin/env node' | cat - dist/cli/setup.js > tmp && mv tmp dist/cli/setup.js && chmod +x dist/cli/setup.js"
```

### package.json bin entry (spec R3.7, R9.1):
Add to `package.json`:
```json
"bin": {
  "@toady00/open-mardi-gras": "dist/cli/setup.js"
}
```

{"description":"## What

## Why

The package needs to ship the `opencode/` directory, expose the CLI setup command, and ensure the CLI has a proper shebang. The README must document the new plugin and its prerequisites so users know how to set it up.

## Implementation

### package.json changes:

1. **bin entry** (spec R9.1, R3.7): Add:
   ```json
   "bin": {
     "@toady00/open-mardi-gras": "dist/cli/setup.js"
   }
   ```

2. **files array** (spec R9.2): Add `"opencode/"` to the existing `files` array:
   ```json
   "files": [
     "dist/",
     "opencode/",
     "LICENSE",
     "README.md",
     "CHANGELOG.md"
   ]
   ```

3. **build script shebang** (spec R3.8): Update the `build` script to prepend `#!/usr/bin/env node` to `dist/cli/setup.js` after compilation:
   ```json
   "build": "tsc -p tsconfig.build.json && echo '#!/usr/bin/env node' | cat - dist/cli/setup.js > tmp && mv tmp dist/cli/setup.js && chmod +x dist/cli/setup.js"
   ```
   This is needed because `tsc` does not emit shebangs.

### README updates (spec R9.3):

Add documentation sections for:

1. **BeadsPlugin usage**: Show how to add `BeadsPlugin()` to an opencode config alongside `ThenChainingPlugin()`. Example:
   ```typescript
   import { ThenChainingPlugin, BeadsPlugin } from '@toady00/open-mardi-gras'
   export default {
     plugins: [ThenChainingPlugin(), BeadsPlugin()]
   }
   ```

2. **Prerequisites**: Document that `bd` (beads CLI) and `yq` must be on `$PATH` for BeadsPlugin to work. Note that these are only required if using BeadsPlugin — ThenChainingPlugin has no external dependencies.

3. **Setup command**: Document `npx @toady00/open-mardi-gras setup` and what it does (copies workflow files to `.opencode/`, writes `.workflow.yaml`).

4. **Plugin compatibility**: Note that both plugins can be used together safely (the PluginCoordinator handles coordination automatically).

## Where

- Modified: `package.json`
- Modified: `README.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `package.json` has `bin` entry pointing to `dist/cli/setup.js`
- [ ] `package.json` `files` array includes `opencode/`
- [ ] `package.json` `build` script includes shebang prepend step
- [ ] `dist/cli/setup.js` starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node` after `bun run build`
- [ ] `npm pack --dry-run` shows `opencode/` directory with all 14 files
- [ ] README documents BeadsPlugin usage and configuration
- [ ] README documents `bd` and `yq` prerequisites
- [ ] README documents the setup command invocation
- [ ] README notes that `bd`/`yq` are only needed for BeadsPlugin"}

{"id":"omg-lkv.5","title":"CLI setup command (src/cli/setup.ts)","description":"## What

Create the CLI entry point at `src/cli/setup.ts` that copies shipped opencode files into the user's project `.opencode/` directory.

## Why

Users need a single command (`npx @toady00/open-mardi-gras setup`) to install all the workflow files (commands, agents, skills, prompts) into their project. This is the primary onboarding mechanism for the beads workflow.

## Implementation

### Entry point (spec R3.1, R3.9):
`src/cli/setup.ts` is the main CLI script. It parses `process.argv`:
- If first arg is `setup`, run the setup logic
- Otherwise (no args or unrecognized arg), print usage message and `process.exit(1)`

### Setup logic (spec R3.2–R3.6):

1. **Resolve paths**: The package's `opencode/` directory is at `path.resolve(__dirname, '../../opencode')` (relative to `dist/cli/setup.js`). The destination is `path.resolve(process.cwd(), '.opencode')`.

2. **Copy files** (R3.2): Copy all 14 files from the package's `opencode/` tree to the project's `.opencode/` tree, preserving subdirectory structure:
   - `opencode/commands/*` → `.opencode/commands/*`
   - `opencode/agents/*` → `.opencode/agents/*`
   - `opencode/skills/omg-commands/SKILL.md` → `.opencode/skills/omg-commands/SKILL.md`
   - `opencode/skills/omg-epics/SKILL.md` → `.opencode/skills/omg-epics/SKILL.md`
   - `opencode/prompts/*` → `.opencode/prompts/*`

3. **Create directories** (R3.3): Use `fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })` before each copy.

4. **Overwrite existing** (R3.4): Use `fs.copyFileSync()` which overwrites by default.

5. **Write .workflow.yaml** (R3.5): Write to `path.resolve(process.cwd(), '.workflow.yaml')`:
   ```yaml
   specs:
     directory: docs/specs
   ```

6. **Print summary** (R3.6): Print each file copied, the package version (read from `package.json` via `path.resolve(__dirname, '../../package.json')`), and next steps: "Add BeadsPlugin() to your opencode config".

### Edge case (spec Edge Cases → Setup):
The setup command only writes specific files. It must NOT delete or modify any files it doesn't own in `.opencode/`.

### Note:
The `package.json` changes (bin entry, build script shebang step, files array) are handled in the "Package.json updates" task (omg-lkv.8). This task only creates the TypeScript source file.

- [ ] Summary of actions and package version printed to stdout
- [ ] Running CLI with no arguments prints usage and exits with code 1
- [ ] `bun run build` succeeds","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"toady00@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T16:04:19Z","created_by":"Brandon Dennis","updated_at":"2026-03-01T16:07:54Z"}

{"id":"omg-lkv.1","title":"PluginCoordinator singleton (src/coordination.ts)","description":"## What

Create `src/coordination.ts` exporting a `PluginCoordinator` class and a module-level singleton `coordinator`.

## Why

Both BeadsPlugin and ThenChainingPlugin need to coordinate around session events. The coordinator is the shared dependency that both plugins import. It must exist before either plugin can reference it.

## Implementation

Create `src/coordination.ts` with the following API:

```typescript
// Interface for what the coordinator needs from a chain state manager.
// Avoids importing the concrete ChainStateManager class, keeping the
// coordination module decoupled from then-chaining internals.
export interface ChainStateProvider {
  hasActiveChain(sessionID: string): boolean
}

export class PluginCoordinator {
  registerChainState(provider: ChainStateProvider): void
  isChainActive(sessionID: string): boolean
  onChainComplete(sessionID: string, callback: () => void): void
  notifyChainComplete(sessionID: string): void
}

export const coordinator: PluginCoordinator
```

### Key behaviors (spec R2.5–R2.8):

- `registerChainState(provider)`: Stores a reference to the provider. Called by ThenChainingPlugin during init. The `ChainStateManager` class already satisfies the `ChainStateProvider` interface (it has `hasActiveChain(sessionID)`).
- `isChainActive(sessionID)`: Queries all registered providers to check if any chain is active for the given session. Returns `false` if no providers are registered (safe when only BeadsPlugin is loaded).
- `onChainComplete(sessionID, callback)`: Queues a callback to fire when `notifyChainComplete` is called for that session. **Deduplicates**: if a callback is already queued for a session, replace it (don't stack). This handles R2.8 (multiple compactions during a chain → only one re-injection).
- `notifyChainComplete(sessionID)`: Fires and removes all queued callbacks for the session. Must be a no-op when no callbacks are queued (safe to call unconditionally).

### Internal state:
- `private providers: ChainStateProvider[]` — registered chain state providers
- `private pendingCallbacks: Map<string, () => void>` — one callback per session (deduplication)

### Coordination state is per-session:
A then-chain in session A must not block beads injection in session B. The `isChainActive()` and callback maps are keyed by sessionID.

### Export from `src/index.ts`:
Add: `export { coordinator, PluginCoordinator } from './coordination.js'`
Also export the `ChainStateProvider` type.

## Where

- New file: `src/coordination.ts`
- Modified: `src/index.ts` (add exports)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `coordinator` is a module-level singleton (both plugins get the same instance)
- [ ] `ChainStateProvider` interface is exported (not the concrete ChainStateManager)
- [ ] `isChainActive()` returns `false` when no providers are registered
- [ ] `onChainComplete()` deduplicates callbacks per session (R2.8)
- [ ] `notifyChainComplete()` is a no-op when no callbacks are queued
- [ ] Per-session isolation: chain in session A doesn't affect session B
- [ ] Unit tests in `src/coordination.test.ts` cover all methods and edge cases
- [ ] `bun run build` succeeds","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"toady00@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T16:02:48Z","created_by":"Brandon Dennis","updated_at":"2026-03-01T16:07:03Z"}

new_value: Created src/coordination.ts with PluginCoordinator class and module-level singleton. Exports coordinator, PluginCoordinator, and ChainStateProvider from index. All 10 unit tests pass covering: no-provider fallback, multi-provider queries, callback dedup, per-session isolation, no-op notify. Build succeeds.
{"assignee":"Brandon Dennis","status":"in_progress"}

{"id":"omg-lkv.3","title":"Wire ThenChainingPlugin into PluginCoordinator","description":"## What

Update the ThenChainingPlugin to register its ChainStateManager with the PluginCoordinator and call `notifyChainComplete()` at the right lifecycle points.

## Why

The ThenChainingPlugin must participate in coordination so the BeadsPlugin knows when chains are active and when they complete. Without this, beads context injection can race with chain advancement (the original bug described in spec R2).

## Implementation

### Register ChainStateManager (spec R2.5):
In `src/plugins/then-chaining.ts`, after creating the `ChainStateManager` (line 37), call:
```typescript
import { coordinator } from '../coordination.js'
coordinator.registerChainState(stateManager)
```

### Notify on chain completion (spec R2.7):
In `src/plugins/then-chaining.ts`, in the `event` handler for `session.idle` (around line 177), after `executor.processNext(sessionID)` returns `false`, call:
```typescript
if (!dispatched) {
  coordinator.notifyChainComplete(sessionID)
  await logger('info', 'Then chain: completed')
}
```

### Notify on chain interruption (spec R2.7):
In `src/plugins/then-chaining.ts`, at every call site where `stateManager.interrupt(sessionID)` is called (lines 107 and 138), add `coordinator.notifyChainComplete(sessionID)` immediately after. There are two interrupt sites:
1. Line ~107: user command with no then-chain interrupts active chain
2. Line ~138: user command with a then-chain interrupts and replaces active chain

**Important**: Do NOT modify `src/then-chaining/state.ts` or `src/then-chaining/executor.ts` to add coordinator calls. The coordinator is a plugin-level concern — keep it in the plugin file where the call sites already exist. The state and executor modules should remain coordinator-unaware.

### Safety:
`notifyChainComplete()` is a no-op when no callbacks are queued, so it's safe to call unconditionally. No conditional logic needed.

## Where

### Factory function pattern (spec R1.6):
The current plugin is exported as `const BeadsPlugin: Plugin = async ({ client, $ }) => { ... }`. Convert it to a factory function: `export function BeadsPlugin(): Plugin` that returns the async plugin function. This matches ThenChainingPlugin's pattern (see `src/plugins/then-chaining.ts`).

### Event hooks (spec R1.1–R1.5):

1. **`chat.message`** (R1.2, R1.5): On first user message per session, run `bd sync` then `bd prime`, inject output as synthetic user message wrapped in `<beads-context>` tags followed by `<beads-guidance>` block. Skip if `bd prime` returns empty. Skip if beads context already exists in session (scan recent messages for `<beads-context>` tag).

2. **`session.compacted`** (R1.3): Re-inject beads context. **Must coordinate with PluginCoordinator** — if a then-chain is active, defer injection via `coordinator.onChainComplete()` (R2.4, R2.6).

3. **`session.idle`** (R1.4): Run `bd sync` quietly. This is always safe regardless of chain state (R2.2) — no coordination needed.

### Coordinator integration (spec R2.1–R2.4, R2.6):
- Import `coordinator` from `../coordination.js`
- Before injecting context on `session.compacted`, check `coordinator.isChainActive(sessionID)`

### Error handling (spec R1.7):
All `bd` and shell invocations must be wrapped in try/catch. On error, log via `client.app.log` and continue. The plugin must never crash OpenCode.

{"id":"omg-lkv.15","title":"Setup command has no error handling for file copy failures","description":"## Location

`src/cli/setup.ts` lines 33-41

## Problem

The setup command's file copy loop has no try/catch:

```typescript
for (const file of FILES_TO_COPY) {
  const src = join(sourceRoot, file)
  const dest = join(destRoot, file)
  mkdirSync(dirname(dest), { recursive: true })
  copyFileSync(src, dest)
  console.log(\`  copied: .opencode/\${file}\`)
  copied++
}
```

If any file fails to copy (e.g., permission denied on the destination, source file missing from a corrupted install), the command crashes with an unhandled exception and a raw Node.js stack trace. The user gets no indication of which files were successfully copied and which weren't.

## Impact

Poor user experience on setup failure. Partial setup state with no clear recovery path.

## Fix

Wrap the loop body in try/catch, log which file failed, and either continue (best-effort) or exit with a clear error message and non-zero exit code.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"toady00@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:43:57Z","created_by":"Brandon Dennis","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:43:57Z"}

`package.json` line 25, build script:

```
echo '#!/usr/bin/env node' | cat - dist/cli/setup.js > tmp && mv tmp dist/cli/setup.js && chmod +x dist/cli/setup.js
```

## Problem

The build script writes to a file called `tmp` in the project root. This has two minor issues:

1. If the build is interrupted between `> tmp` and `mv tmp`, a stale `tmp` file is left in the project root.
2. The name `tmp` could collide with an existing file (unlikely but possible).

## Suggested Fix

Use `mktemp` or write to `dist/cli/setup.js.tmp` instead:

```
echo '#!/usr/bin/env node' | cat - dist/cli/setup.js > dist/cli/setup.js.tmp && mv dist/cli/setup.js.tmp dist/cli/setup.js && chmod +x dist/cli/setup.js
```

Also consider adding `tmp` to `.gitignore` as a safety measure.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","owner":"toady00@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:43:47Z","created_by":"Brandon Dennis","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:43:47Z"}

{"id":"omg-lkv.17","title":"session.compacted handler does not clear injectedSessions for the session","description":"## Location

`src/plugins/beads.ts` lines 167-186

## Problem

When `session.compacted` fires, the handler re-injects beads context but does NOT clear the session from `injectedSessions`. This is actually correct for the current flow (compaction re-injects directly), but there's a subtle issue:

After compaction, the `chat.message` handler will still see the session in `injectedSessions` and skip injection. This is fine because compaction already re-injected. However, if the compacted re-injection was deferred (chain active) and then the chain completes but the deferred callback fails silently, the session would have stale context with no way to re-inject on the next message.

This is a minor robustness concern, not a bug in the happy path. The current behavior is correct for normal operation.

## Impact

Very low — only affects the edge case where deferred injection fails AND the user continues the session. The session would have no beads context but the plugin would think it was already injected.

## Suggested Fix

Consider clearing the sessionID from `injectedSessions` on compaction so the next `chat.message` can serve as a fallback injection point if the compaction injection fails.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"toady00@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:44:16Z","created_by":"Brandon Dennis","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:44:16Z"}

{"id":"omg-lkv.18","title":"Multiple eslint-disable comments suppress type safety in BeadsPlugin","description":"## Location

`src/plugins/beads.ts` lines 73-77, 140-144

## Problem

The BeadsPlugin has 6 eslint-disable comments suppressing TypeScript safety rules:

1. Lines 73-74: `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-call`, `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-member-access` for \`bd sync\`.quiet()
2. Lines 76-77: Same rules plus `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-assignment` for \`bd prime\`.text()
3. Lines 140-141: `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-assignment`, `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any`, `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-member-access` for message parts access
4. Lines 143-144: `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-call`, `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-member-access` for parts.some()

The shell template literal (\`$\`) type issues (items 1-2) are likely due to the `@opencode-ai/plugin` shell type not being fully typed. The message parts access (items 3-4) is due to the OpenCode SDK not exposing `parts` in the message type.

## Impact

These suppressions are pragmatic given the SDK's type limitations, but they hide potential runtime errors. The `(msg as any).parts || (msg.info as any).parts` pattern on line 141 is particularly fragile — if the SDK changes its message shape, this breaks silently.

## Suggested Fix

Consider adding a typed helper function with a single `@ts-expect-error` or `as unknown as` cast at the boundary, rather than scattering inline suppressions. This would centralize the type-unsafe code.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","owner":"toady00@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:44:28Z","created_by":"Brandon Dennis","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:44:28Z"}

{"id":"omg-5dp","title":"Fix BeadsPlugin: replace session.prompt injection with system.transform","description":"BeadsPlugin uses client.session.prompt() with noReply:true to inject beads context on first message. This creates a user message that races with ThenChainingPlugin's chain registration and triggers extra LLM turns, breaking then-chains.

Fix: Replace the session.prompt injection mechanism with experimental.chat.system.transform hook. This appends beads context to the system prompt on every LLM call, eliminating the race condition entirely. The beads context becomes system instructions rather than a user message.

1. Remove chat.message hook for injection (and the session.prompt call)
2. Add experimental.chat.system.transform hook that appends beads context
3. Run bd prime once per session (or after compaction), cache the output

5. Keep session.idle bd sync and compaction refresh logic
6. Simplify coordinator usage (no longer need to defer injection during chains since system prompt is always safe)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"toady00@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T19:24:24Z","created_by":"Brandon Dennis","updated_at":"2026-03-01T19:24:24Z"}

{"description":"# Replace `bd sync` With Dolt-Native Commands

## Overview

The beads CLI removed the `bd sync` command entirely as part of the migration
from JSONL-based sync to Dolt-native storage. Running `bd sync` now returns
`Error: unknown command "sync"`. This project references `bd sync` in plugin
source code, shipped workflow files, documentation, and agent instructions.
All references must be updated to use the Dolt-native equivalents.

### Background

`bd sync` used to serialize the SQLite database to `issues.jsonl`, commit
it to git, and optionally push to a git remote. With Dolt as the sole
backend, this pipeline is gone. Dolt provides its own version control:

- **`bd dolt commit`** -- commits pending changes in the Dolt working set.
  With `auto-commit: on` (the default), every `bd` write auto-commits, so
  an explicit `bd dolt commit` is a cheap no-op. With `auto-commit: batch`
  or `off`, it flushes accumulated changes. Exits 0 even when there is
  nothing to commit. Works regardless of whether a Dolt remote is configured.

- **`bd dolt push`** -- pushes Dolt commits to a configured Dolt remote.
  Exits 1 if no remote is configured. This is the user's responsibility
  (or handled by auto-push if the user configures a remote).

### Replacement Principle

| Old intent                  | Old command  | New command       |
|-----------------------------|--------------|-------------------|
| Flush local state           | `bd sync`    | `bd dolt commit`  |
| Push to remote              | `bd sync`    | `bd dolt push`    |

The plugin must only flush local state. It must never push on behalf of the
user. Workflow commands and agent instructions should use the appropriate
replacement depending on the intent of the original `bd sync` call.

---

## Scope

Changes are limited to files tracked in this repository, excluding:

- **`.opencode/` development directory** — contains `bd-` prefixed local
  workflow files that also reference `bd sync`. These are maintained by
  hand and are out of scope for this spec.
- **`.beads/issues.jsonl` and `.beads/backup/`** — auto-generated JSONL
  files containing historical issue descriptions that reference `bd sync`.
  These are not modified.

### Files in scope

| File | Nature of change |
|------|-----------------|
| `src/plugins/beads.ts` | Runtime code: replace `bd sync` calls |
| `src/plugins/beads.test.ts` | Test mocks: update command strings |
| `AGENTS.md` | Agent instructions: update quick ref and landing workflow |
| `README.md` | Documentation: update BeadsPlugin description |
| `.beads/README.md` | Auto-generated: replace with fresh `bd init` output |
| `opencode/commands/omg-work.md` | Workflow: replace sync steps |
| `opencode/commands/omg-decompose.md` | Workflow: replace sync step |
| `opencode/commands/omg-cleanup.md` | Workflow: replace sync step and description |
| `opencode/skills/omg-commands/SKILL.md` | CLI reference: replace sync section |

---

## Requirements

### R1: Plugin runtime (`src/plugins/beads.ts`)

**R1.1**: In `fetchBeadsContext()`, replace `await $`bd sync`.quiet()` with
`await $`bd dolt commit`.quiet()`. The purpose is to flush any uncommitted
Dolt working set changes before running `bd prime`, ensuring the prime
output reflects the latest state.

**R1.2**: In the `session.idle` event handler, replace
`await $`bd sync`.quiet()` with `await $`bd dolt commit`.quiet()`.

**R1.3**: Update the idle handler's error handling. The current code uses a
bare `catch {}` that silently swallows all errors. Replace it with a
try/catch that always logs a warning via the plugin logger, matching the
pattern already used in `fetchBeadsContext`. Do not attempt to classify
errors — log all of them as warnings.

**R1.4**: Update the JSDoc comment at the top of `fetchBeadsContext` --
currently says "Run `bd sync` then `bd prime`", should say "Run
`bd dolt commit` then `bd prime`".

**R1.5**: Update the module-level doc comment (lines 8-9) -- currently says
"Automatic `bd sync` on session idle", should describe the behavior
abstractly: "Automatic flush of pending beads state on session idle".

**R1.6**: Update the inline comments at lines 124-125 -- currently says
"bd sync is idempotent, cheap (ms), and a no-op when beads isn't
initialized", should say "bd dolt commit is idempotent, cheap, and a no-op
when auto-commit is on or beads isn't initialized".

### R2: Plugin tests (`src/plugins/beads.test.ts`)

**R2.1**: Update mock command matching from `bd sync` to `bd dolt commit`.

**R2.2**: Update the test name "runs bd sync on session idle" to reflect the
new command.

### R3: Agent instructions (`AGENTS.md`)

**R3.1**: In the Quick Reference block, replace `bd sync` and also fix the
stale `--status in_progress` flag. The updated block should read:
```
bd ready              # Find available work
bd show <id>          # View issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim work
bd close <id>         # Complete work
bd dolt commit        # Commit pending beads changes
bd dolt push          # Push beads to remote (if configured)
```

**R3.2**: In the Landing the Plane workflow (step 4), replace:
```bash
git pull --rebase
bd sync
git push
git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"
```
with:
```bash
git pull --rebase
bd dolt push || true  # push beads to Dolt remote if configured
git push
git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"
```
The `|| true` prevents agents from getting stuck when no Dolt remote is
configured. `bd dolt commit` is not needed here because auto-commit handles
it, and the prior steps (closing issues, etc.) already triggered commits.
The `git status` verification line is preserved — it validates the git push,
not the beads sync.

### R4: README (`README.md`)

**R4.1**: Update the BeadsPlugin description (line 116). Replace:
> The plugin also runs `bd sync` on session idle to keep beads state in sync.

with an abstract description:
> The plugin also flushes pending beads state on session idle.

### R5: `.beads/README.md`

**R5.1**: Replace the contents of `.beads/README.md` with the output from a
fresh `bd init`. Procedure:

1. Create a temp directory and run `bd init` in it.
2. Copy the generated `.beads/README.md` from the temp directory over the
   existing `.beads/README.md` in the repo.
3. Clean up the temp directory.

The current file references `bd sync`, JSONL storage, and "Intelligent JSONL
merge resolution". The fresh version should reference `bd dolt push`, Dolt
storage, and "Dolt-native three-way merge resolution". Verify the generated
file contains `bd dolt push` and does not contain `bd sync` before copying.

### R6: Shipped workflow commands (`opencode/commands/`)

**R6.1**: `omg-work.md` line 14 -- replace `bd sync` with `bd dolt commit`
in the "epic complete" step: `bd epic close-eligible && bd dolt commit`.

**R6.2**: `omg-work.md` line 23 -- remove the `bd sync` step (step 8)
entirely. After closing a bead, auto-commit handles persistence. The next
iteration of the loop starts with `bd ready` which reads from the committed
state. Removing this step tightens the loop. Renumber the remaining step:
"Go back to step 1" becomes step 8 (was step 9).

**R6.3**: `omg-decompose.md` lines 29-33 -- replace the `bd sync` + git add
block. The intent is to ensure spec file and beads state are committed before
decomposition. Replace `bd sync` with `bd dolt commit`:
```
bd dolt commit
git add $1 .beads/
git status
```

**R6.4**: `omg-cleanup.md` line 9 -- replace `bd sync` with `bd dolt commit`.
Also update the frontmatter description from "cleanup, doctor, sync" to
"cleanup, doctor, commit".

### R7: Shipped skills reference (`opencode/skills/omg-commands/SKILL.md`)

**R7.1**: Replace the "Sync" section (lines 94-99) with an updated
reference:
```
## Sync

```
bd dolt commit                         # Commit pending beads changes
bd dolt push                           # Push to Dolt remote (if configured)
```
```

Remove the `bd sync --flush-only` line entirely -- there is no equivalent
and the concept no longer applies.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `bd sync` does not appear in any in-scope file
- [ ] Plugin calls `bd dolt commit` (not `bd sync`) in both locations
- [ ] Plugin idle handler logs warnings on all `bd dolt commit` failures
- [ ] All tests pass (`bun test`)
- [ ] Build succeeds (`bun run build`)
- [ ] Lint passes (`bun run lint`)
- [ ] `grep -r "bd sync" src/ opencode/ AGENTS.md README.md .beads/README.md` returns zero matches
- [ ] `.beads/README.md` contains `bd dolt push` (positive check for fresh content)
- [ ] AGENTS.md Quick Reference uses `--claim` (not `--status in_progress`)
- [ ] `omg-work.md` has exactly 8 numbered steps (step 8 = "Go back to step 1")

## Edge Cases

- **No Dolt remote configured**: `bd dolt commit` works fine (local-only).
  `bd dolt push` in AGENTS.md uses `|| true` to avoid blocking agents.
- **Auto-commit on (default)**: `bd dolt commit` is a no-op. No harm, no
  performance concern.
- **Auto-commit batch/off**: `bd dolt commit` flushes accumulated changes.
  This is the case where the explicit commit call matters most.
- **bd not installed**: Plugin logs a warning (R1.3). The `fetchBeadsContext`
  caller returns null, so the plugin degrades gracefully. The idle handler
  logs and continues.
- **Dolt server not running**: `bd dolt commit` will fail. Plugin logs a
  warning (R1.3) and continues.
- **`bd init` output changes**: R5.1 generates the file dynamically rather
  than hardcoding content. If `bd init` changes its README template in the
  future, the generated file will reflect those changes automatically.

## Open Questions

None.

- [ ] `bd sync` does not appear in any in-scope file
- [ ] Plugin calls `bd dolt commit` (not `bd sync`) in both locations
- [ ] Plugin idle handler logs warnings on all `bd dolt commit` failures
- [ ] Idle handler error test verifies a warning was logged (not just no-throw)
- [ ] All tests pass (`bun test`)
- [ ] Build succeeds (`bun run build`)
- [ ] Lint passes (`bun run lint`)
- [ ] `grep -r "bd sync" src/ opencode/ AGENTS.md README.md .beads/README.md` returns zero matches
- [ ] `.beads/README.md` contains `bd dolt push` (positive check for fresh content)
- [ ] AGENTS.md Quick Reference uses `--claim` (not `--status in_progress`)
- [ ] `omg-work.md` has exactly 8 numbered steps (step 8 = "Go back to step 1")

## Edge Cases

- **No Dolt remote configured**: `bd dolt commit` works fine (local-only).
  `bd dolt push` in AGENTS.md uses `|| true` to avoid blocking agents.
- **Auto-commit on (default)**: `bd dolt commit` is a no-op. No harm, no
  performance concern.
- **Auto-commit batch/off**: `bd dolt commit` flushes accumulated changes.
  This is the case where the explicit commit call matters most.
- **bd not installed**: Plugin logs a warning (R1.3). The `fetchBeadsContext`
  caller returns null, so the plugin degrades gracefully. The idle handler
  logs and continues.
- **Dolt server not running**: `bd dolt commit` will fail. Plugin logs a
  warning (R1.3) and continues.
- **`bd init` output changes**: R5.1 generates the file dynamically rather
  than hardcoding content. If `bd init` changes its README template in the
  future, the generated file will reflect those changes automatically.

## Open Questions

None.

{"id":"omg-yf9","title":"Spec: Replace bd sync with Dolt-native commands","description":"# Replace `bd sync` With Dolt-Native Commands

## Overview

The beads CLI removed the `bd sync` command entirely as part of the migration
from JSONL-based sync to Dolt-native storage. Running `bd sync` now returns
`Error: unknown command "sync"`. This project references `bd sync` in plugin
source code, shipped workflow files, documentation, and agent instructions.
All references must be updated to use the Dolt-native equivalents.

### Background

`bd sync` used to serialize the SQLite database to `issues.jsonl`, commit
it to git, and optionally push to a git remote. With Dolt as the sole
backend, this pipeline is gone. Dolt provides its own version control:

- **`bd dolt commit`** -- commits pending changes in the Dolt working set.
  With `auto-commit: on` (the default), every `bd` write auto-commits, so
  an explicit `bd dolt commit` is a cheap no-op. With `auto-commit: batch`
  or `off`, it flushes accumulated changes. Exits 0 even when there is
  nothing to commit. Works regardless of whether a Dolt remote is configured.

- **`bd dolt push`** -- pushes Dolt commits to a configured Dolt remote.
  Exits 1 if no remote is configured. This is the user's responsibility
  (or handled by auto-push if the user configures a remote).

### Replacement Principle

| Old intent                  | Old command  | New command       |
|-----------------------------|--------------|-------------------|
| Flush local state           | `bd sync`    | `bd dolt commit`  |
| Push to remote              | `bd sync`    | `bd dolt push`    |

The plugin must only flush local state. It must never push on behalf of the
user. Workflow commands and agent instructions should use the appropriate
replacement depending on the intent of the original `bd sync` call.

---

## Scope

Changes are limited to files tracked in this repository, excluding:

- **`.opencode/` development directory** — contains `bd-` prefixed local
  workflow files that also reference `bd sync`. These are maintained by
  hand and are out of scope for this spec.
- **`.beads/issues.jsonl` and `.beads/backup/`** — auto-generated JSONL
  files containing historical issue descriptions that reference `bd sync`.
  These are not modified.

### Files in scope

| File | Nature of change |
|------|-----------------|
| `src/plugins/beads.ts` | Runtime code: replace `bd sync` calls |
| `src/plugins/beads.test.ts` | Test mocks: update command strings |
| `AGENTS.md` | Agent instructions: update quick ref and landing workflow |
| `README.md` | Documentation: update BeadsPlugin description |
| `.beads/README.md` | Auto-generated: replace with fresh `bd init` output |
| `opencode/commands/omg-work.md` | Workflow: replace sync steps |
| `opencode/commands/omg-decompose.md` | Workflow: replace sync step |
| `opencode/commands/omg-cleanup.md` | Workflow: replace sync step and description |
| `opencode/skills/omg-commands/SKILL.md` | CLI reference: replace sync section |

---

## Requirements

### R1: Plugin runtime (`src/plugins/beads.ts`)

**R1.1**: In `fetchBeadsContext()`, replace `await $`bd sync`.quiet()` with
`await $`bd dolt commit`.quiet()`. The purpose is to flush any uncommitted
Dolt working set changes before running `bd prime`, ensuring the prime
output reflects the latest state.

**R1.2**: In the `session.idle` event handler, replace
`await $`bd sync`.quiet()` with `await $`bd dolt commit`.quiet()`.

**R1.3**: Update the idle handler's error handling. The current code uses a
bare `catch {}` that silently swallows all errors. Replace it with a
try/catch that always logs a warning via the plugin logger, matching the
pattern already used in `fetchBeadsContext`. Do not attempt to classify
errors — log all of them as warnings.

**R1.4**: Update the JSDoc comment at the top of `fetchBeadsContext` --
currently says "Run `bd sync` then `bd prime`", should say "Run
`bd dolt commit` then `bd prime`".

**R1.5**: Update the module-level doc comment (lines 8-9) -- currently says
"Automatic `bd sync` on session idle", should describe the behavior
abstractly: "Automatic flush of pending beads state on session idle".

**R1.6**: Update the inline comments at lines 124-125 -- currently says
"bd sync is idempotent, cheap (ms), and a no-op when beads isn't
initialized", should say "bd dolt commit is idempotent, cheap, and a no-op
when auto-commit is on or beads isn't initialized".

### R2: Plugin tests (`src/plugins/beads.test.ts`)

**R2.1**: Update mock command matching from `bd sync` to `bd dolt commit`.

**R2.2**: Update the test name "runs bd sync on session idle" to reflect the
new command.

### R3: Agent instructions (`AGENTS.md`)

**R3.1**: In the Quick Reference block, replace `bd sync` and also fix the
stale `--status in_progress` flag. The updated block should read:
```
bd ready              # Find available work
bd show <id>          # View issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim work
bd close <id>         # Complete work
bd dolt commit        # Commit pending beads changes
bd dolt push          # Push beads to remote (if configured)
```

**R3.2**: In the Landing the Plane workflow (step 4), replace:
```bash
git pull --rebase
bd sync
git push
git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"
```
with:
```bash
git pull --rebase
bd dolt push || true  # push beads to Dolt remote if configured
git push
git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"
```
The `|| true` prevents agents from getting stuck when no Dolt remote is
configured. `bd dolt commit` is not needed here because auto-commit handles
it, and the prior steps (closing issues, etc.) already triggered commits.
The `git status` verification line is preserved — it validates the git push,
not the beads sync.

### R4: README (`README.md`)

**R4.1**: Update the BeadsPlugin description (line 116). Replace:
> The plugin also runs `bd sync` on session idle to keep beads state in sync.

with an abstract description:
> The plugin also flushes pending beads state on session idle.

### R5: `.beads/README.md`

**R5.1**: Replace the contents of `.beads/README.md` with the output from a
fresh `bd init`. Procedure:

1. Create a temp directory and run `bd init` in it.
2. Copy the generated `.beads/README.md` from the temp directory over the
   existing `.beads/README.md` in the repo.
3. Clean up the temp directory.

The current file references `bd sync`, JSONL storage, and "Intelligent JSONL
merge resolution". The fresh version should reference `bd dolt push`, Dolt
storage, and "Dolt-native three-way merge resolution". Verify the generated
file contains `bd dolt push` and does not contain `bd sync` before copying.

### R6: Shipped workflow commands (`opencode/commands/`)

**R6.1**: `omg-work.md` line 14 -- replace `bd sync` with `bd dolt commit`
in the "epic complete" step: `bd epic close-eligible && bd dolt commit`.

**R6.2**: `omg-work.md` line 23 -- remove the `bd sync` step (step 8)
entirely. After closing a bead, auto-commit handles persistence. The next
iteration of the loop starts with `bd ready` which reads from the committed
state. Removing this step tightens the loop. Renumber the remaining step:
"Go back to step 1" becomes step 8 (was step 9).

**R6.3**: `omg-decompose.md` lines 29-33 -- replace the `bd sync` + git add
block. The intent is to ensure spec file and beads state are committed before
decomposition. Replace `bd sync` with `bd dolt commit`:
```
bd dolt commit
git add $1 .beads/
git status
```

**R6.4**: `omg-cleanup.md` line 9 -- replace `bd sync` with `bd dolt commit`.
Also update the frontmatter description from "cleanup, doctor, sync" to
"cleanup, doctor, commit".

### R7: Shipped skills reference (`opencode/skills/omg-commands/SKILL.md`)

**R7.1**: Replace the "Sync" section (lines 94-99) with an updated
reference:
```
## Sync

```
bd dolt commit                         # Commit pending beads changes
bd dolt push                           # Push to Dolt remote (if configured)
```
```

Remove the `bd sync --flush-only` line entirely -- there is no equivalent
and the concept no longer applies.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `bd sync` does not appear in any in-scope file
- [ ] Plugin calls `bd dolt commit` (not `bd sync`) in both locations
- [ ] Plugin idle handler logs warnings on all `bd dolt commit` failures
- [ ] All tests pass (`bun test`)
- [ ] Build succeeds (`bun run build`)
- [ ] Lint passes (`bun run lint`)
- [ ] `grep -r "bd sync" src/ opencode/ AGENTS.md README.md .beads/README.md` returns zero matches
- [ ] `.beads/README.md` contains `bd dolt push` (positive check for fresh content)
- [ ] AGENTS.md Quick Reference uses `--claim` (not `--status in_progress`)
- [ ] `omg-work.md` has exactly 8 numbered steps (step 8 = "Go back to step 1")

## Edge Cases

- **No Dolt remote configured**: `bd dolt commit` works fine (local-only).
  `bd dolt push` in AGENTS.md uses `|| true` to avoid blocking agents.
- **Auto-commit on (default)**: `bd dolt commit` is a no-op. No harm, no
  performance concern.
- **Auto-commit batch/off**: `bd dolt commit` flushes accumulated changes.
  This is the case where the explicit commit call matters most.
- **bd not installed**: Plugin logs a warning (R1.3). The `fetchBeadsContext`
  caller returns null, so the plugin degrades gracefully. The idle handler
  logs and continues.
- **Dolt server not running**: `bd dolt commit` will fail. Plugin logs a
  warning (R1.3) and continues.
- **`bd init` output changes**: R5.1 generates the file dynamically rather
  than hardcoding content. If `bd init` changes its README template in the
  future, the generated file will reflect those changes automatically.

## Open Questions

None.
